structure_type.monolayer: One isolated periodic layer with out-of-plane vacuum.
structure_type.bilayer: Two layers in one periodic simulation cell.
structure_type.trilayer: Three layers in one periodic simulation cell.
structure_type.few-layer: Four or more explicitly resolved layers, short of a bulk repeat.
structure_type.heterostructure: A stack containing compositionally distinct layers.

classes.pristine: No intentional substitution, vacancy, intercalation, or adsorbate.
classes.optimized: Geometry was relaxed by a documented computational method.
classes.homobilayer: Both layers derive from the same parent monolayer.
classes.heterobilayer: The two layers derive from different parent monolayers.
classes.stacking-state: One member of a registered stacking family.
classes.switching-path-member: One image or endpoint in a switching path.
classes.intercalated: Atoms or molecules are inserted between host layers or sites.
classes.substituted: One or more sites are intentionally substituted.
classes.vacancy: One or more sites are intentionally removed.
classes.adsorbed: Atoms or molecules are adsorbed on a surface.
classes.doped: Charge or compositional doping is intentionally represented.
classes.strained: A documented strain is applied relative to a parent structure.
classes.reconstructed: The structure is a documented reconstruction of a parent.
classes.experimental: Geometry is derived directly from an experimental structure.
classes.computed: Geometry is generated or relaxed computationally.

phenomena.polar: A polar structure or state under the stated convention.
phenomena.nonpolar: A nonpolar structure or state under the stated convention.
phenomena.sliding-ferroelectric: Ferroelectric states connected primarily by relative layer sliding.
phenomena.intercalation-ferroelectric: Ferroelectric behavior controlled by an intercalant configuration.
phenomena.displacive-ferroelectric: Ferroelectric states connected primarily by atomic displacements.
phenomena.fractional-ferroelectric: A fractional-quantized ferroelectric mechanism under a documented theory.
phenomena.multistate-ferroelectric: More than two symmetry-distinct polar states are documented.
phenomena.antiferroelectric: Opposing local polar order with a documented switching definition.
phenomena.piezoelectric: A verified piezoelectric response is attached.
phenomena.topological: A verified topological material label is attached.

evidence_level.not-evaluated: No claim is made for the requested phenomenon.
evidence_level.candidate: A rule-based or generated candidate without complete screening.
evidence_level.screened: Passed a documented computational screening workflow.
evidence_level.dft-verified: The claim is supported by documented first-principles calculations.
evidence_level.experimental: The claim is supported by a cited experiment.

quality_flags.bulk-monolayer-composition-mismatch: The mapped bulk parent and monolayer have different reduced compositions; the relationship is retained as source metadata and requires scientific review.
quality_flags.source-metadata-cell-multiplicity-differ: The archive metadata atom count and canonical CIF use different cell multiplicities while retaining the same reduced composition.
quality_flags.source-metadata-composition-mismatch: The canonical CIF composition differs from the archive metadata row; metadata-derived scientific properties are omitted.

relationship_type.derived-from: The record is derived from another structure.
relationship_type.bulk-parent-of: A bulk parent linked to a derived or relaxed monolayer record.
relationship_type.parent-monolayer: A monolayer used to build a stack.
relationship_type.stacking-partner: The other layer in a heterostructure.
relationship_type.host: The host structure for an intercalated record.
relationship_type.switching-pair: A state connected through a documented switching process.
relationship_type.path-member: A neighboring or parent switching-path record.
